title: Model record row
description: One row of data/models.csv. Every model comparison table in docs/ is generated from these rows. A value that the responsible organisation has not published is recorded as 'Not publicly disclosed'; a value that is claimed but unverified is 'Insufficient independent evidence'. Empty cells are a validation error.

model_id.description: The provider's exact API model string, recorded verbatim including any date or version suffix. Where no API string exists, the exact weights repository identifier.

model_name.description: Human-readable model name as the provider writes it.

release_date.description: Date of first general availability or public release.

status.description: Availability status as of source_date. This field ages quickly and is always read together with source_date.

deprecation_date.description: Announced retirement date where the provider has published one.

open_weights.description: True where model parameters are downloadable under any licence. Open weights is not open source: see glossary.md.

license.description: Exact licence name, for example 'Apache-2.0' or the provider's named community licence. 'Proprietary' for closed models served only by API.

license_url.description: Link to the licence text, or 'Not available'.

architecture.description: Architecture family as disclosed, for example 'decoder-only transformer', 'sparse mixture-of-experts transformer', 'hybrid attention and state-space'. Not inferred from behaviour.

parameters_total.description: Total parameter count in billions. Recorded only where disclosed. Never estimated from benchmark behaviour or from inference cost.

parameters_active.description: Parameters used per token in billions. For a dense model this equals parameters_total.

expert_count.description: Number of experts per mixture-of-experts layer, or 'Not applicable' for a dense model.

modalities_input.description: Pipe-separated accepted input modalities from: text, image, audio, video, document.

modalities_output.description: Pipe-separated produced output modalities from: text, image, audio, video.

context_window_tokens.description: Advertised maximum context in tokens. Advertised, not measured: measured effective context is recorded in data/context-windows.csv.

max_output_tokens.description: Advertised maximum output length in tokens.

tokenizer.description: Tokenizer name or family where disclosed.

reasoning_mode.description: Whether extended reasoning is unavailable, caller-selectable, always applied, offered as a distinct model, or undisclosed.

tool_use.description: Structured tool-call support as documented by the provider.

agent_capability.description: Documented agentic features, for example computer use or long-horizon tool loops, or 'Not publicly disclosed'. Capability claims here are provider documentation, not measured performance; measured performance belongs in data/benchmarks.csv.

quantization_support.description: Quantization formats published by the provider or by the weights repository, pipe-separated. 'Not applicable' for API-only models.

deployment_options.description: Pipe-separated from: first_party_api, cloud_marketplace, self_hosted, on_device, private_deployment.

hardware_requirements.description: Minimum accelerator memory and configuration for self-hosting at a stated precision, or 'Not applicable' for API-only models. The precision must be named in the value.

evidence_grade.description: Grade of the source supporting this row. Specifications from an official model card are B; provider performance claims are C.

source_id.description: Must resolve to a row in data/sources.csv.

source_date.description: Date the source stated these values. Every status and specification claim is read relative to this date.

notes.description: Conflicts between sources, superseding records, and any qualification a reader needs. 'None' where there is nothing to record.
